meta_title: For Founders: project management to Solve tutorial hell (Intermediate Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to project management and overcome tutorial hell. A Intermediate-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Project Management for Founders: Escape Tutorial Hell
content.seo_description: Stop watching, start shipping. Use project management frameworks, Antigravity, and Claude Code to build revenue-generating SaaS products as an AI builder.
content.answer_block: Tutorial hell thrives on passive consumption. To escape, shift from 'learning' to 'shipping' by mapping features to a T-shaped project management framework. Use Antigravity for rapid scaffolding and Claude Code to execute logic implementation, ensuring every hour spent results in a deployable, revenue-ready SaaS component.
content.intro:
You have spent countless hours consuming YouTube tutorials and documentation, yet your production environment remains a ghost town. This is the 'Tutorial Hell' trap—a cycle of infinite preparation that masks a fear of real-world deployment. You are a founder, not a student, and your code needs to solve problems, not just pass exercises.

True mastery in AI building comes from constraints and systematic execution. By treating your development process as a project management challenge rather than a coding lesson, you force yourself to build, break, and iterate. It is time to stop watching others build the future and start engineering your own path to revenue.

steps.title: Define the Atomic MVP
steps.description: Break your SaaS idea into three core features. Avoid scope creep by strictly defining what 'done' looks like for each feature.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a lean product requirements document for a [SaaS Concept] focusing only on the core value proposition. List 3 atomic features.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write 'Create a README.md outlining the MVP features defined in the PRD.'

steps.title: Automated Scaffolding
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate the boilerplate code so you aren't fighting configuration files for hours.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a secure, scalable Next.js and Supabase boilerplate with authentication and Stripe integration.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Initialize project structure based on generated boilerplate code.'

steps.title: Feature-Driven Logic
steps.description: Implement the core logic using Claude Code's iterative feedback loop to ensure functionality meets your requirements.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write the server-side logic for [Feature Name] including error handling and database interaction.
steps.claude_code_command: claude build 'Implement the logic for [Feature Name] and run unit tests.'

steps.title: State & Data Flow Mapping
steps.description: Visualize your data schema to prevent technical debt early. A clean schema is the backbone of a maintainable SaaS.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Prisma schema for a multi-tenant SaaS application with subscription tiers.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Apply database migrations and verify schema integrity.'

steps.title: Deployment & Validation
steps.description: Push to production immediately. Validation only happens when real users interact with your code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a deployment checklist for a Vercel-hosted production app.
steps.claude_code_command: claude deploy 'Push to staging and run smoke tests.'

pro_tips: Treat your IDE like a production environment; never write code that isn't connected to a GitHub issue.
Use Claude Code to write your own unit tests before writing the implementation to enforce TDD.
If you find yourself stuck for more than 30 minutes, pivot the feature to a simpler implementation rather than debugging deep technical rabbit holes.
Externalize your state management early; never hardcode configuration values that should be in environment variables.
Ship the 'ugly' version first; revenue is the only true metric of a successful build.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is live, implement a feedback loop by integrating a simple analytics tool to track user interactions and iterate based on actual usage patterns.
content.conversion_block: Stop building for the sake of learning and start building for the sake of profit. Our community is packed with founders just like you who have successfully escaped the tutorial trap to launch profitable SaaS products. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
